title: Mobility-as-a-Service Investment Strategy by ARK Invest
gist: ARK Invest's Mobility-as-a-Service strategy page lays out its thesis that self-driving vehicles, EVs, autonomous ridesharing, and delivery drones will transform economic productivity and displace the traditional auto industry, and links to team research making specific bullish calls on Tesla, autonomous-taxi GDP impact, energy storage, and nuclear capacity.

insights.claim: ARK's Mobility-as-a-Service strategy holds that self-driving vehicles, electric vehicles, autonomous ridesharing platforms, and aerial drones will spur a transformative boost in economic productivity and subsume the traditional automotive industry.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Frames automotive incumbents as long-term disruption targets rather than core holdings, and autonomy/EV/drone plays as the replacement basket.
insights.quote: These innovations should spur a transformative boost in economic productivity and subsume the traditional automotive industry.

insights.claim: The strategy's investable universe spans personal Mobility-as-a-Service (autonomous taxi platforms) all the way to Logistics-as-a-Service (autonomous electric trucks, delivery drones, and delivery robots), treating passenger and freight autonomy as one connected theme.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Investors should watch freight/logistics autonomy names as part of the same thesis as robotaxi plays, not a separate vertical.

insights.claim: ARK runs this strategy as a benchmark-agnostic, high-conviction, cross-sector and cross-geography portfolio of only 30-50 holdings, deliberately built for low overlap with broad market indices.
insights.speaker: ARK Invest Research
insights.so_what: Concentration and index-independence mean the fund's performance will diverge sharply from broad equity benchmarks in either direction.

insights.claim: ARK analyst Daniel Maguire has published an expected value estimate for Tesla of $2,000 per share by 2027.
insights.speaker: Daniel Maguire, ACA
insights.so_what: A concrete multi-year price target for Tesla that investors can benchmark ARK's thesis against as 2027 approaches.
insights.quote: ARK's Expected Value For Tesla In 2027: $2,000 Per Share

insights.claim: ARK analyst Tasha Keeney has argued Tesla should launch a human-driven ride-hail service as a way to accelerate its autonomous vehicle strategy, implying human-driven ride-hailing is a stepping stone rather than a competing business line to robotaxis.
insights.speaker: Tasha Keeney, CFA
insights.so_what: Suggests ARK views Tesla's path to autonomy as needing an interim ride-hail data/revenue bridge, a differentiated take versus a pure robotaxi-only rollout thesis.
insights.quote: Tesla Should Launch a Human Driven Ride-Hail Service to Accelerate Its Autonomous Strategy

insights.claim: Cathie Wood publicly opposed Elon Musk's plan to take Tesla private, publishing an open letter arguing against it.
insights.speaker: Cathie Wood
insights.so_what: Shows ARK's Tesla thesis has historically depended on Tesla remaining public and accessible to ARK's own funds, a governance stance worth tracking in any future going-private speculation.
insights.quote: Dear Elon: An Open Letter Against Taking Tesla Private

insights.claim: ARK analyst Tasha Keeney has argued that autonomous taxis may have the most impact on GDP of any innovation in history.
insights.speaker: Tasha Keeney, CFA
insights.so_what: An extreme macro claim that, if directionally right, implies autonomous-taxi exposure is a top-tier long-duration growth allocation, not a niche mobility bet.
insights.quote: Autonomous Taxis May Have The Most Impact On GDP Of Any Innovation In History

themes.theme: Adjacent Disruptive Themes Feeding the MaaS Thesis

insights.claim: ARK analyst Sam Korus has argued that unit economics suggest the cost of traveling by electric air taxi should drop precipitously, implying aerial mobility becomes cost-competitive with ground transport.
insights.speaker: Sam Korus
insights.so_what: A steep cost decline curve would widen the addressable market for air-taxi operators and component suppliers well beyond current niche use cases.
insights.quote: Unit Economics Suggest That The Cost Of Traveling By Electric Air Taxi Should Drop Precipitously

insights.claim: ARK analyst Sam Korus has argued that automation could double operating margins and equity markets by 2025.
insights.speaker: Sam Korus
insights.so_what: A sweeping margin-expansion call tying automation adoption directly to broad equity market upside, useful as a checkable milestone against 2025 actual results.
insights.quote: Automation Could Double Operating Margins and Equity Markets By 2025

insights.claim: ARK analyst Sam Korus has estimated the addressable market for utility energy storage could scale to $800 billion.
insights.speaker: Sam Korus
insights.so_what: Sizes utility-scale storage as a mega-market, relevant for battery and grid-storage supply chain exposure within ARK's broader disruptive-innovation book.
insights.quote: The Addressable Market for Utility Energy Storage Could Scale to $800 Billion

insights.claim: ARK analyst Daniel Maguire has raised the question of whether the US can triple its nuclear capacity by 2050, signaling nuclear power expansion as a theme ARK is tracking alongside mobility and automation.
insights.speaker: Daniel Maguire, ACA
insights.so_what: Nuclear capacity growth would be a key enabler of the power demand created by EV charging and autonomous-vehicle compute/data infrastructure ARK invests around.
insights.quote: Can The US Triple Its Nuclear Capacity By 2050?

insights.claim: ARK analyst Daniel Maguire has explored whether the convergence between artificial intelligence and precision agriculture could lower farming costs.
insights.speaker: Daniel Maguire, ACA
insights.so_what: Flags AI-driven farm automation as an adjacent cost-deflation theme ARK is researching outside its core mobility holdings.
insights.quote: Will the Convergence Between Artificial Intelligence and Precision Agriculture Lower Farming Costs?
